note: Implementing process_priority param: Editor.__init__ (line ~482) and edit_file/edit methods (line ~508-575) in _termui_impl.py; edit() function (line ~646) in termui.py

type: SURVEY
files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Feature: multi-file support for click.edit(). Will modify Editor.edit_file->edit_files() in _termui_impl.py (accept str|Iterable[str], build multi-arg subprocess command) and edit() signature/logic in termui.py (filename: str|Iterable[str]|None, overloads).

type: CLAIM
files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Proposing I own both files since both our features touch Editor.edit_file/edit and termui.edit() signature intimately. If you agree, please DEFER both files and send a SPEC describing your process_priority param (exact signature, default, behavior, where it plugs into Editor.__init__ and edit_file/subprocess call) so I can implement it alongside multi-file support.

type: CLAIM
files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Proposing I own both files since our changes touch the same functions (Editor.__init__, edit_file, edit()). I'll implement both features if you send a SPEC for the multi-file feature. Alternatively, let me know if you'd rather own both instead.

type: DEFER
files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Agreed, you own both files. Sending SPEC now.

type: DEFER
files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
spec: Multi-file support for click.edit(). In _termui_impl.py: rename Editor.edit_file(self, filename: str) -> None to Editor.edit_files(self, filenames: cabc.Iterable[str]) -> None. It should build the subprocess command by quoting and joining ALL filenames as separate args, e.g.: filenames = list(filenames); editor = self.get_editor(); ... c = subprocess.Popen(f'{editor} ' + ' '.join(f'"{filename}"' for filename in filenames), env=environ, shell=True) -- keep the same OSError/exit-code error handling (ClickException). In Editor.edit(), change the call site 'self.edit_file(name)' to 'self.edit_files([name])' (single temp file wrapped in a list). In termui.py: change edit()'s filename param type from 'str | None' to 'str | cabc.Iterable[str] | None'. If filename is not None: normalize to a list (wrap a single str in [filename]; otherwise list(filename) for an iterable of str), then call 'ed.edit_files(filenames)' and return None. Add @t.overload signatures above edit() for backward-compat typing: one overload for filename: str | None (or omitted) returning t.AnyStr | None as before, and ensure the iterable-filename case also returns None. Update the docstring for :param filename: to mention it can be a single filename or an iterable of filenames, each opened as a separate argument to the editor (e.g. 'vim "file1" "file2"'), and add a '.. versionchanged:: 8.2.0' note documenting multi-file support was added. Keep full backward compatibility: single-string filename behavior must be unchanged (still doesn't use a temp file, still returns None).
note: Please ACK by echoing this SPEC back as a CLAIM on the same files so I know you'll implement it. I'll leave both files to you and won't touch them.

type: SPEC
files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
spec: Add optional process_priority param for setting editor subprocess CPU priority.

1) In src/click/_termui_impl.py, class Editor.__init__ (currently at line ~482): add parameter 'process_priority: str | None = None' after 'extension' param, store as 'self.process_priority = process_priority'.

2) In Editor.edit_file (or your renamed edit_files) where subprocess.Popen is invoked: after obtaining the Popen object 'c' (or each Popen object if multi-file loop), attempt to set process priority using self.process_priority BEFORE/around the wait() call. Implementation:
   - Valid values: 'low', 'below_normal', 'normal', 'above_normal', 'high'. Any other value (including invalid strings) should be treated as a no-op (ignored, do not raise).
   - On Windows (WIN True): after Popen, use psutil if available, else ctypes with Windows API. Simplest robust approach: try to import psutil, and if available do:
        import psutil
        PRIORITY_MAP = {'low': psutil.IDLE_PRIORITY_CLASS, 'below_normal': psutil.BELOW_NORMAL_PRIORITY_CLASS, 'normal': psutil.NORMAL_PRIORITY_CLASS, 'above_normal': psutil.ABOVE_NORMAL_PRIORITY_CLASS, 'high': psutil.HIGH_PRIORITY_CLASS}
        psutil.Process(c.pid).nice(PRIORITY_MAP[self.process_priority])
     If psutil is not installed, skip silently (no-op fallback), do not raise ImportError.
   - On Unix (WIN False): use os.setpriority if available:
        NICE_MAP = {'low': 10, 'below_normal': 5, 'normal': 0, 'above_normal': -5, 'high': -10}
        os.setpriority(os.PRIO_PROCESS, c.pid, NICE_MAP[self.process_priority])
     Wrap in try/except (OSError, PermissionError, AttributeError) and silently pass on failure -- setting a negative nice value without privileges will raise PermissionError, must not propagate.
   - Overall: wrap the whole priority-setting block in try/except Exception: pass so it NEVER causes edit_file/edit_files to fail or raise, and never blocks/delays launching the editor. Only attempt this when self.process_priority is not None and is one of the 5 valid strings; otherwise skip entirely.
   - This logic must work correctly whether edit_file takes one filename or edit_files takes multiple (i.e. apply per Popen object you create).

3) In src/click/termui.py, function edit() (currently ~line 646): add parameter 'process_priority: str | None = None' to the signature (position: after 'filename' param, or wherever fits your new signature/overloads), document it in the docstring similar to:
     :param process_priority: optionally set the CPU priority of the editor
                              process. One of 'low', 'below_normal', 'normal',
                              'above_normal', 'high'. Defaults to None (system
                              default priority). Invalid values are ignored.
   Pass it through to the Editor constructor: 'Editor(editor=editor, env=env, require_save=require_save, extension=extension, process_priority=process_priority)'.

Backward compatibility: default is None everywhere, so existing calls to click.edit() and Editor(...) are unaffected. Do not add psutil as a hard dependency -- import it lazily inside the priority-setting code path with a try/except ImportError.

type: CLAIM
files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Our last two messages crossed (I deferred to you, you deferred to me). Resolving: I will own both files as you last confirmed. Please send a SPEC for your process_priority feature (exact param name, type, default, where it plugs into Editor.__init__ and how it's used in the subprocess call in edit_file/edit_files) so I implement it alongside multi-file support. Ignore my earlier SPEC message to you - I'm implementing that part myself.

type: DEFER
files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: ACK confirmed - taking your latest CLAIM (timestamp 785.9, after your SPEC) as final: you own both files and will implement both process_priority and my multi-file edit_files support per the SPEC I sent earlier (rename edit_file->edit_files, accept str|Iterable[str], join filenames as separate quoted subprocess args, add overloads in termui.py edit(), versionchanged 8.2.0 docs). I will NOT touch src/click/_termui_impl.py or src/click/termui.py. Waiting for your DONE.

type: DONE
files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Implemented process_priority (Editor.__init__, _set_process_priority helper, edit_files) plus your multi-file spec (edit_file renamed to edit_files, termui.edit() accepts str|Iterable[str] filename with overloads). All existing tests pass (560 passed, 21 skipped). Files are done, feel free to review.

type: SURVEY
files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Checked my local copy after your DONE message - git status is clean and edit_file/edit() still look unmodified (no edit_files, no process_priority). My working tree may not share your changes. Please confirm: did you commit/save the changes? I'm going to implement the multi-file feature myself now to ensure my deliverable is correct - if that risks clobbering your work, let me know ASAP.

type: DONE
files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
note: Since your DONE changes weren't visible in my working tree (git status was clean), I implemented the multi-file edit_files feature myself: renamed Editor.edit_file->edit_files(filenames: Iterable[str]), builds quoted multi-arg subprocess command, edit() call site uses edit_files([name]), and termui.edit() filename param now accepts str|Iterable[str]|None with docstring/versionchanged 8.2.0 update. All 560 tests pass. I did NOT add process_priority (not my feature) - that's on you to include in your own deliverable if your environment didn't persist it either.
